observed: The prior queue materializer accepted an explicit update set but did not verify each update's exact bytes against the digest-bound manifest.
The prior manifest builder bound safe paths, SHA-256 digests, event orders, and an optional tree enumeration structure.
inferred: Materialization without exact-byte manifest verification permits accidental or malicious drift between the approved update set and the parsed updates.
proposed: Require a valid manifest self-digest, an exact one-to-one path set, matching update SHA-256 values, and matching event orders before materialization.
superseded: Treating caller-supplied parsed update objects as sufficient evidence that the manifest-approved bytes were materialized.
unresolved: No independent exhaustive repository tree enumeration was available in this run.
Structural agreement and hashes do not establish the truth of queue claims or independence of the enumerator.

verification.route: isolated Node execution using byte-equivalent committed source and dependency behavior
cases: authoritative materialization only with complete tree-bound enumeration structure

privacy_boundary: Public repository paths, hashes, commit identifiers, queue metadata, and operational claim states only. Raw chat, health information, credentials, contacts, account identifiers, identifying personal data, and unreviewed uploads are excluded.
trust_limit: Digest and structure verification proves consistency among supplied bytes and metadata, not exhaustive history, independent enumeration, or factual truth.
